purpose: Empty-state landing card shown by GitManagerView when no repositories are open (gitRepos.length === 0). Renders a centered card with a branch icon, a 'Git Manager' title, descriptive text, and a single primary 'Open Repository' button that invokes api.ideSelectFolder() (reusing the IDE view's folder picker) then dispatches GIT_OPEN_REPO with id=String(Date.now()), the picked path, and the trailing folder name as the tab label. Mirrors IDEWelcome.jsx visually.
gotchas: Reuses api.ideSelectFolder (from the IDE namespace) rather than a git-specific picker — if that IPC is renamed, both GitWelcome and RepoTabs break.
Repo id is generated via String(Date.now()) — opening two repos in the same millisecond would collide. Acceptable for human-driven use but not for automated tests.
ideSelectFolder may return either a string or an object with .path — the helper handles both shapes; do not assume one or the other when extending.
If user cancels the picker, folderPath is falsy and the function returns early without error — no toast or feedback.
patterns: Empty-state pattern: git-manager-empty container + git-manager-empty-icon + title + text + primary action button.
Folder name extraction: replace trailing slashes, split on / or \, take last segment — handles cross-platform paths.
conventions: Inline SVG icons (GitBranchIcon, FolderOpenIcon) at top of file.
Console.error for unexpected failures, no UI error state.
